title: Variation 1: First-time conversation
dialogue:
A: Hi! How's it going?
B: Hi! I'm good, thanks. And you?
A: I'm fine. Nice to meet you!
B: Nice to meet you too! What do you do?
A: I work in a bookstore. What about you?
B: I’m a teacher. I love it!

title: Variation 2: Problem-solving discussion
dialogue:
A: We have a problem with the project.
B: Yes, I know. What do you think?
A: I think we can try a different approach.
B: Good idea! What should we do?
A: Let's find a solution together.
B: Okay! I will make a list of ideas.

title: Variation 3: Quick decision under time pressure
chunk_focus: What do you want to do?
dialogue:
A: We need to decide quickly! The movie starts soon.
B: Yes! What do you want to do?
A: Should we eat first or go to the movie?
B: Let's eat after. I want to see the movie!
A: Okay! Let's go with that.

title: Variation 4: Polite disagreement
dialogue:
A: I think we should start the meeting early.
B: I see your point, but I prefer to start later.
A: Why? Is there a reason?
B: Yes, I don’t agree because more people will be there later.
A: That's a good idea. Maybe we can compromise.

title: Variation 5: Making a request
dialogue:
A: Hi! Can you help me?
B: Sure! What do you need?
A: I need your help with my homework.
B: Of course! What subject is it?
A: It’s math. Could you please explain this problem?
B: Yes, I can!

title: Variation 6: Follow-up after a misunderstanding
chunk_focus: I think there was a misunderstanding.
I didn't mean to confuse you.
dialogue:
A: I think there was a misunderstanding earlier.
B: Yes, I didn't mean to confuse you.
A: What did you mean?
B: I meant the meeting is next week, not this week.
A: Oh! Let’s clarify that together.

title: Variation 7: Planning for tomorrow
dialogue:
A: What time should we meet tomorrow?
B: Let’s meet at 10 AM.
A: Okay! I will bring the papers.
B: Great! I will bring the snacks.
A: Sounds good! See you then!

title: Variation 8: Asking for advice
chunk_focus: What do you think I should do?
dialogue:
A: Hi! I need some advice.
B: Sure! What do you think I should do?
A: I want to change my job.
B: Maybe you should look for something in your field.
A: That’s a good idea! Thank you!

title: Variation 9: Comparing two options
dialogue:
A: I have two shirts. Which one do you prefer?
B: I like the blue one.
A: Why?
B: This one is better because it looks nice.
A: I like this one more too! Let's buy it.

title: Variation 10: Wrapping up and next steps
chunk_focus: Let’s summarize what we talked about.
dialogue:
A: Let’s summarize what we talked about.
B: Yes, we discussed the project.
A: Next, we will meet again next week.
B: I will email you the details.
A: Perfect! Thanks for the meeting!
